recent_decisions.title: Training updates and platform investigation noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted training updates and requested an investigation into replacing the MiPod platform for Code of Conduct training. No vote recorded.
key_data_points: No budget currently available for new platform

motions.text: That the training updates and briefing note be noted; that the Head of Member Services be requested to contact the Learning and Development Manager to investigate if another platform for the Code of Conduct training could be procured instead of using MiPod; and that options be sought via the Council’s Service Improvement Board for potential investment into a different learning platform as detailed at (ii) above.

recent_decisions.title: Complaints update noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted updates on four complaints, with three rejected or closed and one handled by Neighbourhood Services. No vote recorded.
key_data_points: 1 complaint closed due to lack of public interest

recent_decisions.title: Government consultation response noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the government's response to a consultation on strengthening standards and conduct frameworks for local authorities. No vote recorded.
key_data_points: Future sanctions expected to be strengthened

motions.text: That the government’s response to the consultation on ‘Strengthening the Standards and Conduct Framework for Local Authorities in England’ be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Register of Interests dip sampling results noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the dip sampling of three councillors' Interest Forms, with amendments made to two forms. A date column will be added for gifts/hospitality. No vote recorded.
key_data_points: Date column to be added for gifts/hospitality

motions.text: That the Committee noted the dip-sampling, and subsequent amendments made on the Interest Forms pertaining to Councillors Crowe and Potter; that the Committee noted the dip-sampling of the form relating to Parish Councillor Platt of Goosnargh Parish Council; and that the Head of Member Services be requested to revise Section 2e of the Interest Forms going forward, to include a column with the date a gift or hospitality was received.

recent_decisions.title: Agreed shortlist and assessment process for Director of Environment and Property
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the shortlist of candidates and the assessment process for the Director of Environment and Property post, including written exercises, presentations, and interviews. No vote counts were recorded.
key_data_points: Shortlist of candidates agreed for Director of Environment and Property
Assessment includes written exercise, presentation, and Member Panel interview
Process assisted by Northwest Employers representative
Urgent recruitment due to tight timeline

motions.text: That the Committee agreed the proposed shortlist of candidates to be invited for further assessment, as well as the format and content of the assessment exercises and Member Interview Panel.

recent_decisions.title: Exclusion of press and public from meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee resolved to exclude the press and public from the meeting due to exempt information under the Local Government Act 1972, with the public interest in maintaining the exemption outweighing disclosure.
key_data_points: Exclusion based on paragraphs 1 and 3 of Schedule 12A to the Local Government Act 1972
Public interest in maintaining exemption outweighed disclosure
Decision made to protect sensitive recruitment process

motions.text: That the public be excluded from this meeting during consideration of the following item of business on the grounds that there is likely to be disclosure of exempt information which is described in paragraphs 1 and 3 of Schedule 12A to the Local Government Act 1972

recent_decisions.title: Minutes of previous meeting noted and signed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted and signed the minutes of the previous meeting held on 8 December 2025 as a correct record.
key_data_points: Minutes of 8 December 2025 meeting noted and signed

motions.text: That the minutes of a meeting of the Chief Officer Employment Committee held on 8 December 2025 be noted and signed as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Council approves minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council noted and signed the minutes from the previous meeting held on 11 December 2025 without any objections.
key_data_points: Minutes from 11 December 2025 meeting were noted and signed

recent_decisions.title: Council condemns US President's remarks on UK military
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council agreed to issue a statement rejecting the US President's comments about UK military service in Afghanistan, expressing support for veterans and active service personnel.
key_data_points: UK lost over 450 personnel in Afghanistan
Council to issue statement of support for veterans

motions.text: The Council should make a statement through the Leader to reassure veterans and those in active service that the Council fully supports them.

recent_decisions.title: Approved Cenotaph Portland Stone Replacement
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved £100,000 for Cenotaph repairs to prevent accidents and improve aesthetics. Contract awarded to preferred supplier.
key_data_points: Repairs to prevent slips, trips, and falls
Works to be completed within budget

recent_decisions.title: Authorised Highway Agreements for Active Travel Schemes
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet authorised agreements for East-West and North-South Cycle Links and Illuminate and Integrate Part 2, delegating authority to the Interim Director of Environment and Property.
key_data_points: Agreements for East-West and North-South Cycle Links
Section 38/Section 278 agreements with Lancashire County Council
Delegation to Interim Director of Environment and Property
Supports active travel and public realm schemes

recent_decisions.title: Noted Decision Making Plan
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Decision Making Plan, which outlines key decisions and those intended to be made in private.
key_data_points: Details key decisions and private decisions

recent_decisions.title: Refused application for retail units and apartments at 454-456 Blackpool Road
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously refused the application for retail units and apartments, citing harm to residential amenity and area character. All 10 members voted in favor of refusal.
key_data_points: Application refused for over-intensification of site
Cited Preston Local Plan Policy AD1(a)

motions.text: That the application be refused for the following reason: The proposed development by virtue of the size and scale of the proposed building and level of occupancy, would lead to an over-intensification of the use of the site, which would harm residential amenity and the character and appearance of the area contrary to the Preston Local Plan Policy AD1(a)

recent_decisions.title: Approved change of use for Running Pump Hotel to four dwellings
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the change of use for the Running Pump Hotel to four dwellings, with 6 members in favor and 4 against.
key_data_points: Included partial demolition and first floor extension
Subject to conditions outlined in the report

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to conditions outlined in the report and the additional informative listed in the Late Changes report.

recent_decisions.title: Approved reserved matters for 108 dwellings at Land south of Bartle Lane
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the reserved matters for 108 dwellings, with 8 members in favor and 2 abstaining.
key_data_points: Part of hybrid application for up to 195 dwellings
Subject to conditions outlined in the report

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to conditions outlined in the report.

recent_decisions.title: Approved change of use for 6 Lambert Road to HMO or residential institution
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the change of use for 6 Lambert Road to a seven-bed HMO or residential institution, with 7 members in favor, 1 against, and 1 abstaining.
key_data_points: 7 members voted in favor, 1 against, 1 abstained
Included demolition of conservatory and erection of outbuilding
Subject to conditions outlined in the report

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to conditions outlined in the report.

recent_decisions.title: Committee receives update on council finances and LGR
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee received an update on council finances and Local Government Reorganisation (LGR), noting a stable financial position and £1m set aside for LGR work.

Draft three-year settlement figure received on 17 December 2025
Preston settlement better than most authorities in Lancashire

recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the GDPR compliance update, including 41 data breaches in 2024/25, all due to human error except two.

recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the LGO complaints report, with 16 complaints in 2024/25, none upheld.

recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the progress on implementing audit recommendations from the 2023/24 accounts.
